challenges.title: Relative Positioning with Offsets
challenges.description: Write a CSS rule that targets `.nudged` and sets `position` to `relative`, `top` to `10px`, and `left` to `20px`. This shifts the element from its normal position without affecting surrounding elements. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert '.nudged' in css, 'Should target .nudged'
assert 'position' in css.lower(), 'Should set the position property'
assert 'relative' in css.lower(), 'Should use position: relative'
assert 'top' in css.lower(), 'Should set top offset'
assert 'left' in css.lower(), 'Should set left offset'
assert '10px' in css.lower(), 'Should set top to 10px'
assert '20px' in css.lower(), 'Should set left to 20px'
hints: position: relative moves the element relative to where it would normally be.
Use top, right, bottom, left to offset. Positive top moves it down, positive left moves it right.

challenges.title: Absolute Overlay with Z-Index
challenges.description: Write two CSS rules: (1) target `.wrapper` and set `position` to `relative` (this creates the containing block), (2) target `.overlay` and set `position` to `absolute`, `top` to `0`, `left` to `0`, `width` to `100%`, `height` to `100%`, `background-color` using `rgba()` with alpha `0.7`, and `z-index` to `10`. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert '.wrapper' in css, 'Should target .wrapper'
assert '.overlay' in css, 'Should target .overlay'
assert 'relative' in css.lower(), 'Should use position: relative on wrapper'
assert 'absolute' in css.lower(), 'Should use position: absolute on overlay'
assert 'z-index' in css.lower(), 'Should set z-index'
assert 'rgba(' in css.lower(), 'Should use rgba() for semi-transparent background'
assert 'width' in css.lower(), 'Should set width'
assert 'height' in css.lower(), 'Should set height'
assert '100%' in css, 'Should use 100% for full coverage'
hints: An absolutely positioned element is placed relative to its nearest positioned ancestor.
Set top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; to cover the parent. Use z-index to layer it above other content.
